creator: kevinz5000@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Garrett Wollman from comment #2)
This is an upstream issue. I can provide some context here but this discussion is better handled upstream; this PR should probably be closed.

This particular change was made to provide a working alternative for IPFW that didn't segfault or do other surprising things. The rc.d defaults (particularly blacklisting) led to many frequent crashes when using the old IPFW backend. While this change was not ideal it certainly seemed better than letting SSHGuard segfault on potentially hostile inputs.
